docs.title: BOOK XXIII
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: 5. But Julian disregarded his prudent adviser, and advanced boldly; since no human power or virtue can ever avail to prevent events prescribed by the order of the Fates.

docs.title: Annals, Book 3
docs.author: Tacitus
docs.excerpt: But beneath all this was a vigorous mind, equal to the greatest labours, the more active in proportion as he made a show of sloth and apathy. And so while Maecenas lived, he stood next in favour to him, and was afterwards the chief depository of imperial secrets, and accessory to the
